title: Published npm bin depends on Bun and raw TypeScript sources
reasoning: The package is presented as an npm/Node CLI, but it ships a `.ts` bin entry, emits no JavaScript, assumes Bun-only globals/types, and autostarts the daemon by reusing the current runtime to execute another `.ts` file. In a normal Node installation, especially via the Windows npm shim, the CLI and daemon startup path will try to run raw TypeScript under Node and fail before any command logic runs.
reproduction: Pack/install the package in an environment that has Node but not Bun, then run `glimpse list`. On Windows, the generated `.cmd` shim will invoke Node on `src/cli.ts`, which cannot execute this source as shipped.
recommendation: Publish compiled JavaScript for the CLI and daemon, point `bin` at that emitted entrypoint, and make the daemon spawn path target the compiled JS artifact. If Bun is a hard requirement, declare it explicitly in package metadata and stop presenting the package as a generic Node CLI.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The only included tests exercise utility helpers under Bun. There is no packed-install smoke test, no Node-only execution test, and no Windows/bin-shim coverage.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a release smoke test that packs the module, installs it into a clean temporary project with Node only, and asserts `glimpse list` starts successfully. Add a Windows CI job or equivalent shim test for the published bin.
minimumFixScope: package.json, tsconfig/build output, and the daemon spawn target in src/ipc/client.ts

history.reasoning: The original package.json evidence is no longer present: bin now points to ./dist/cli.mjs and package files include dist. The raw TypeScript bin problem is addressed by tsdown.config.ts, which builds src/cli.ts and src/daemon-main.ts into dist. The current src/ipc/client.ts still has a TypeScript fallback for development, but the bundled path checks ./daemon-main.mjs first, and dist/cli.mjs contains that same bundled daemon-main.mjs target. A Node execution smoke check succeeded with node dist/cli.mjs list, returning ok:true. The added release smoke test also passed and explicitly packs/installs the package with a Node-only PATH and runs node_modules/.bin/glimpse list. tsconfig.json still has Bun types/noEmit, but that is no longer evidence that the published bin runs raw TS because the release path now uses tsdown output. npm pack --dry-run could not be used as additional evidence in this read-only sandbox because prepack/cache writes failed with EPERM.
commands: rg -n "daemonEntrypoint|daemon-main|\.ts|process\.execPath|#!/usr/bin/env node" dist/cli.mjs dist/daemon-main.mjs package.json src/ipc/client.ts test/release-smoke.test.ts
